title: bridge-slack Slack timestamp replay guard accepts timestamps 5 min in the future (9-min replay window)
description: verify_slack_signature in bridge-slack/src/slack.rs uses saturating_abs() (line 111): 'now.saturating_sub(ts).saturating_abs() > 300'. This accepts request timestamps up to ~5 minutes in the future as well as the past, creating a ~9-minute effective replay window (an attacker can capture a request, hold it for up to 4m59s in the future relative to now, and replay it within the next 5 minutes). Slack's own documentation accepts a small future skew but the correct guard is 'abs(now - ts) > 300', which saturating_abs already computes correctly for the past case but silently allows large future timestamps to pass via the symmetric acceptance. The fix is to additionally verify ts <= now + small_fuzz (e.g. 30s) to reject bogus future timestamps.
